converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
William Trelease papers
Trelease, William, 1857-1945.
2560
2.5 cubic feet. 2.5 cubic feet.
[ca. 1862-1945]. Correspondence, clippings, photographs, deeds, drawings, and other material. General incoming personal and professional correspondence, c. 1879-c. 1845; correspondence, clippings, and other items concerning his work in the Missouri Botanical Gardens, his monographs, the naming of Mount Trelease, Colorado, activities of the Society of American Bacteriologists, and other subjects; and photographs and miscellaneous printed matter. Also Trelease's correspondence, deeds, and drawings concerning family cemetery plots in Urbana, Illinois, and St. Louis Missouri. Collection material in English 2088706
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
A. Levine
Date completed:
April 1967
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, June 2003
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Botany at Wisconsin, Washington, and Illinois Universities, 1883-1912; and director, Missouri Botanical Gardens, 1889-1912.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
General incoming personal and professional correspondence, c. 1879-c. 1845; correspondence, clippings, and other items concerning his work in the Missouri Botanical Gardens, his monographs, the naming of Mount Trelease, Colorado, activities of the Society of American Bacteriologists, and other subjects; and photographs and miscellaneous printed matter. Also Trelease's correspondence, deeds, and drawings concerning family cemetery plots in Urbana, Illinois, and St. Louis Missouri.
Cite As:
William Trelease papers, #2560. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Botanists -- United States. Cemeteries -- Missouri -- St. Louis. Cemeteries -- Illinois -- Urbana. Botany -- Study and teaching. Botanical gardens -- Missouri -- St. Louis. University of Washington University of Illinois Missouri Botanical Gardens Society of American Bacteriologists

Riley1883-189432Professional correspondence from Charles Sprague Sargent (1841- )1883-189633aProfessional correspondence from Charles Sprague Sargent (1841- )1897-190233b30 typewritten pp., "Gardens and Botanical Gardens"33bCorrespondence (p.c.) between Trelease, Henry Shaw and W.G. Elliot re: Trelease's employment at Missouri Botanical Garden188541A book of letters, mostly regarding Trelease's retirement from his post at the Missouri Botanical Garden. Many of the letters are from well-known men, such as Roscoe Pound, John Muir, Simon and Susanna Phelps Gage, Liberty Hyde Bailey, David Starr Jordan, and the Comstocks.1904-191242a191242b1914-191742cMissouri Botanical Garden, clippings re: annual reports1892-191243Correspondence and other items pertaining to Trelease's "Agave in the West Indies"1912-1917n.d.45Items pertaining to Mount Trelease1932-1933n.d.46Photographs of the Boyce Thomson Southwestern Arboretum192947Letters and other items pertaining to Trelease's monograph of "American Oaks"1925-193548Miscellaneous correspondence, articles, and receipts1860-191851Miscellaneous correspondence1925-192752192853192954a-b193061a-b1931621932631933641934651935661936-1937671938-1939681940711941[19421944]72Letters of condolence, newspaper clippings194573Undated correspondence74Miscellaneous photographs75Pamphlets76Paperbound book, Memoirs of the National Academy of Science, vol. XX (Washington: Government Printing Office, 1924 ). The whole of this volume compromises "The American Oaks," by William Trelease.19247Additional papers1914-[1940-1945]8
Scope and Contents
These additional papers consist mainly of letters from relatives, friends, colleagues, and ex-students on his 80th (1937) and 83rd (1940) birthdays, and letters of condolence (1945) to his widow and children; also included are Trelease's correspondence, deeds, and drawings concerning family cemetery plots in Urban, Illinois and St. Louis, Missouri; William Trelease, Personal Reminiscences by J. Christian Bay (12 pp. pamphlet, 1945); photographs of Trelease; and related printed matter and newspaper clippings. Ca. 150 pcs.
Correspondents of William Trelease:
John W. Spargo - cousin - professor at Northwestern University
Jonibel Blair - granddaughter
Hillebrand - English professor - Harvard A.B. and Ph.D.
Albert L. Lybyer - professor of history at Illinois
Abram Leon Sacher - president of Brandeis University now
Cecelia Tonton - daughter of H.B. Ward
Kaltienn M. Noyes - widow of W.A. Noyes (chemist)
Leighton - Ill. State Geological Survey
Frank W. Scott - with D.C. Heat, formerly professor at Illinois
Eunice Daniels - widow of Pres. XX (Washington: Government Printing Office, 1924 ). The whole of this volume compromises "The American Oaks," by William Trelease.19247Additional papers1914-[1940-1945]8
Scope and Contents
These additional papers consist mainly of letters from relatives, friends, colleagues, and ex-students on his 80th (1937) and 83rd (1940) birthdays, and letters of condolence (1945) to his widow and children; also included are Trelease's correspondence, deeds, and drawings concerning family cemetery plots in Urban, Illinois and St. Louis, Missouri; William Trelease, Personal Reminiscences by J. Christian Bay (12 pp. pamphlet, 1945); photographs of Trelease; and related printed matter and newspaper clippings. Ca. 150 pcs.
